MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Amy Harrington, Esq. with and for James Lum.

Off Calendar Oct-21-2025 Continued To Feb-17-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Oct-21-2025 Continued To Feb-17-26 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Amy Harrington for administrator James Lum (via Zoom).
Catherine Cross for petitioner Christopher J. Bricker (via Zoom).
David Parnall for Tim Lum and Fuling Lum, who were also present.

The matter was called for hearing on Petition to Enforce Marvin Contract and for Status on Mediation.

The Court inquired as to the status of settlement discussions. Ms. Koss reported that Mr. Parnall had substituted in as counsel for Tim and Fuling Lum in July, and due to scheduling conflicts, the parties had experienced delays in progress. She stated that the parties were coordinating further mediation efforts and requested a 120-day continuance to allow time for continued discussions and discovery. She stated that Judge Victoria Wood had previously served as mediator and had agreed to continue assisting if needed.

The Court encouraged the parties to return to Judge Wood to resolve remaining issues. Ms. Harrington stated administration was proceeding appropriately, and that discovery requests were expected soon. She stated that the matter should be set for trial if future settlement efforts are unsuccessful.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to February 17, 2026, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204 to allow for continued mediation and discovery.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar May-27-2025 Continued To Oct-21-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Amy Harrington for and with James Lum, Personal Representative (via Zoom).
Catherine E. Koss for and with Christopher J. Bricker, Petitioner (via Zoom).

Ms. Harrington reported that the parties had participated in a mediation session with Judge Wood but were unable to settle. Counsel indicated that settlement discussions remain ongoing, but litigation would proceed in the meantime.

Ms. Koss informed the Court that they are at the early stages of discovery and are planning to conduct out-of-state depositions in October. Counsel requested that the matter be continued to a date in late October for a further status conference on discovery and mediation efforts.

Following discussion, the Court continued the matter to October 21, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Apr-01-2025 Continued To May-27-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To (1) Enforce Marvin Contract; (2) Confirm Decedent?S Property To Christopher J. Bricker Pursuant To Marvin Contract And Probate Code Section 850; (3) Enforce Oral Contract To Make A Will; And For (4) Damages For Breach Of Contract; And (5) Constructive Trust; Demand For Evidentiary Hearing
Off Calendar Dec-03-2024 Continued To Apr-01-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (via Zoom):
Amy Harrington for and with James Lum, Proposed Personal Representative.
Catherine Koss for Christopher J. Bricker, Petitioner.
Tim Lum and Fuling Lum are present.

Discussion was held regarding bond requirement for James Lum as a nonresident administrator. Ms. Koss stated she has no objection to James acting as administrator, but proposed a bond amount of $400,000, based on the estimated estate value. Ms. Harrington contested the bond amount proposed by Ms. Koss and suggested a lower figure in line with local rules. The court took the Petition for Letters of Administration under submission for further consideration as to the bond issue.

The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than March 18, 2025. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by March 25, 2025. The Petition to Enforce Marvin Contract, etc. is continued to April 1, 2025, and the Parties are to return on April 1, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, for status of mediation. Counsel were instructed to obtain a copy of the signed order from the court's online register of actions.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)
